step_by_step_reasoning: The transcript describes two main workflows. The first is an interactive search: a web user queries a .NET app on ECS. ECS sanitizes the prompt via Bedrock (Claude 2), vectorizes it via Bedrock (Titan), fetches history from SQL on EC2, queries Elasticsearch on EC2 for 6 jobs, then uses Bedrock (Claude 2) to generate and stream the final response. The second workflow is an offline ingestion process running every 6 hours, grabbing partner jobs into S3, vectorizing them with Bedrock (Titan), and loading them into Elasticsearch on EC2.

graph.name: Healthy Careers GenAI Job Search Engine
graph.notes: GenAI-powered job search engine for healthcare professionals using Claude 2 and Titan embeddings with Elasticsearch on EC2.

nodes.name: Practitioner Client
nodes.notes: Doctors and nurses searching for job opportunities.

nodes.name: .NET App Controller
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Handles orchestration of the chat and search experience.

nodes.name: Claude 2 (Sanitization)
nodes.notes: Checks prompt injection, sanitizes inputs, and converts locations to lat/lon.

nodes.name: Titan (Query Vectorizer)
nodes.notes: Used for quick and cost-effective text embeddings.

nodes.name: SQL Database
nodes.notes: Stores user search and interaction history.

nodes.name: Claude 2 (Response Generation)
nodes.notes: Generates and streams final tailored job responses.

nodes.name: Job Feeds & APIs
nodes.notes: External partner feeds, RSS, and APIs.

nodes.name: Document Store
nodes.notes: Stores raw ingested job posting documents.

edges.notes: Pulls feeds every 6 hours into S3.
